advisory.title: ASA-2024-0012, ASA-2024-0013: CosmosSDK transaction decoding may result in a stack overflow or resource exhaustion
advisory.description: Cosmos SDK joint advisory ASA-2024-0012 / ASA-2024-0013 covers two transaction-decoding bugs. ASA-2024-0012 is an uncontrolled-recursion stack overflow in unknown-protobuf-field detection on deeply nested packets. ASA-2024-0013 is an exponential resource-exhaustion bug in nested UnpackAny processing — wasm contract messages or malicious validator blocks can exhaust CPU and memory, bypassing the max_tx_bytes cap and crashing nodes. The v0.50.10..v0.50.11 patch (commit ba7ac458c9 'Merge commit from fork') limits recursion depth in codec/unknownproto/unknown_fields.go and the unpack any path in codec/types/interface_registry.go and x/tx/decode/unknown.go.

introducingCommits.subject: codec: implement protobuf unknown fields checker (#6557)

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.50.10 release commit, the last vulnerable v0.50 release before the v0.50.11 patch. The introducing commit is the original unknown-protobuf-fields checker (#6557), which the v0.50.11 patch (ba7ac458c9) updates with a recursion depth limit. The patch also touches codec/types/interface_registry.go and x/tx/decode/unknown.go for the UnpackAny path.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag unbounded recursion in codec/unknownproto/unknown_fields.go and unbounded UnpackAny depth in codec/types/interface_registry.go and x/tx/decode/unknown.go that allow deeply nested transactions to stack-overflow or exhaust CPU/memory.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: ASA-2024-0012/0013 (GHSA-8wcc-m6j2-qxvm) was published on 2024-12-16 with patches in cosmos-sdk v0.50.11 and v0.47.15.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.50.10 release commit, the latest tag in the <=0.50.10 vulnerable range on the v0.50 line.
